top10.introHtml: Top 10 picks near Media, PA (ZIP 19063) — now including Cars.com, CarGurus, and AutoTrader. Ranked by value, condition, and fun factor. New this run: 2006 Audi S4 Cabriolet ($7,404, V8, Great Deal), 2004 Mercedes-Benz SL 500 ($9,898, 53K mi, Great Deal), 2004 Audi A4 1.8T ($9,950, 46K mi). All picks confirmed available today.

picks.whyGood: NEW from Cars.com — a 4.2L V8 AWD Audi S4 Cabriolet for $7,404 is the most outrageous value in this entire search. The B7 S4 made 340hp and launched 0-60 in 5.1s. Great Deal badge. 97K miles. No accidents, clean title. Cars that came near $60K new don't usually appear at $7.4K with a Great Deal rating.
picks.whatToVerify: The B7 S4 V8 has known cam follower failure — MUST inspect (early failure destroys the engine). Check for recent cam follower replacement or budget $300 to do it immediately. Oil consumption test. Timing chain condition. AWD Haldex coupling fluid. Convertible top mechanism — full open/close cycle. Budget $500–800 for immediate maintenance.
picks.bottomLine: Highest power-per-dollar in the pool by a wide margin — PPI is non-negotiable but if it passes, this is the steal of the year.

picks.whyGood: The Z3 2.8 gets the rare M52B28 inline-6 — genuinely fun to drive, not just cute. $7,940, 75K miles, No Accidents, Clean Title via Private Seller Exchange, only 14.81 mi away. The 2.8L is a step-change over the base 1.9 four-cylinder. Price-to-fun ratio is unbeatable for a sorted, clean roadster.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate soft top through a full open-close cycle; check headliner and carpet for water staining; inspect rear plastic window for yellowing or delamination; look at rocker panels and front frame rails for rust; cooling system (thermostat, water pump); VANOS rattle on cold start; valve cover gasket seep.
picks.bottomLine: Best-value sorted roadster — inline-6 Z3 with clean history at a price that's hard to argue with.

picks.whyGood: PRICE DROP — down from $9,500 to $8,452. The R52 Cooper S has the Roots-type supercharger which delivers unique fizz that turbocharged cars can't replicate. 65K miles is legitimately low for this era. No Accidents. At $8,452 it's now significantly better value.
picks.whatToVerify: Timing chain tensioner and guide — failure known between 60K–120K on these engines; this one is right at the boundary. Supercharger belt and snout seal. Full soft top operation — R52 electric roof can develop actuator issues. Coolant leaks at electric water pump. Service records.
picks.bottomLine: Fun supercharged drop-top with genuine low miles — price drop makes it the best-value MINI in the pool.

picks.whyGood: NEW from Cars.com — only 53,643 miles on a 2004 SL 500. The R230 SL 500 had a 302hp 5.0L V8 and a retractable aluminum hardtop (Vario-Roof). Cars that listed for ~$85K new don't appear at $9,898 with a Great Deal badge and 53K miles often. Clean title, no accidents.
picks.whatToVerify: ABC (Active Body Control) hydraulic suspension — failure is expensive ($3,000–5,000); check for any suspension warning lights and uneven ride height. Airmatic strut condition. Magic Sky roof mechanism full cycle. V8 timing chain service history. Panoramic roof seal condition.
picks.bottomLine: A genuine luxury V8 roadster at $9,898 — the ABC suspension check is the single most important inspection item.

picks.whyGood: The Z4 is a sharper, more refined roadster than the Z3 — better chassis, better interior, better top mechanism. 88K miles with No Accidents and only 16 mi away. The M54B25 inline-6 is one of BMW's most durable engines. Recent price-drop badge confirms seller motivation.
picks.whatToVerify: Soft top operation and rear window condition; VANOS rattle on cold start; oil leaks at valve cover and pan; cooling system (M54 thermostat, water pump impeller); front subframe bushings; windshield cowl drain for clogging.
picks.bottomLine: More refined than the Z3 at a modest premium — excellent sports roadster, close to home.

picks.whyGood: MAJOR PRICE DROP — down from $11,500 to $9,500. 61K miles on a 1992 C4 is a genuine survivor. LT1 V8, rear-wheel drive, American muscle. No Accidents. At $9,500 this is priced like a driver, not a collector piece.
picks.whatToVerify: C4 pop-up headlight motors (test both), digital dash cluster for dead pixels, LT1 Opti-Spark distributor moisture issues, all rubber weatherstripping (34-year-old rubber), fiberglass body for cracks, convertible top fabric and rear window, AC compressor.
picks.bottomLine: Massive price drop makes this C4 compelling — 61K miles and now priced to move.

picks.whyGood: NEW from Cars.com — only 45,587 miles on a 2004 is extraordinary. The B6 A4 Cabriolet is a handsome, refined soft-top with AWD (quattro). Good Deal badge. At under 46K miles on a 22-year-old car, this is near-museum condition. No accidents, clean title.
picks.whatToVerify: Timing belt is age-overdue regardless of mileage — must replace immediately (budget $400–600). Coolant flange and thermostat housing (known B6 failure points). Boost pressure check on 1.8T. Soft top hydraulic mechanism full cycle. Check for evidence of long-term storage (dry-rotted belts, fuel system issues).
picks.bottomLine: Survivor-condition Audi cabriolet at a fair price — timing belt replacement is mandatory but well worth it.

picks.whyGood: Newly listed. 74K miles, No Accidents — the C70 T5's retractable hardtop gives year-round usability that soft-tops can't match. At $9,995 it's well-priced. The 2.5L turbo five-cylinder makes 227hp and Volvo build quality shows. Among the most practical convertibles at this price.
picks.whatToVerify: Retractable hardtop mechanism — cycle multiple times; check rubber seals at folding points; listen for hydraulic pump effort. Rust at lower rocker panel edge (common C70 spot). Turbo boost and oil consumption. Auto transmission fluid condition.
picks.bottomLine: Stylish all-season hardtop-convertible at excellent mileage — newly listed and freshly priced.

picks.whyGood: 22,000 miles on a 2009 is extraordinary — effectively new-condition. The Saturn Sky uses the Kappa platform, a rear-wheel-drive roadster with genuine sports-car intent. No Accidents. Premium trim adds leather and upgraded audio. At 22K it has none of the typical wear.
picks.whatToVerify: Operate convertible top through full cycle (check motor and seals for sitting-related stiffness). Inspect rubber weather seals for dry-rot from storage. Check tire age — 17-year-old tires at any mileage need replacement. Verify power steering, belts, A/C. Look for storage damage (mice, fuel system).
picks.bottomLine: Unicorn-condition RWD roadster at top of budget — if it checks out mechanically, a once-in-a-decade find.

picks.whyGood: The E93 328i is the best 4-seat drop-top here — a true full convertible with a glass heated rear window. Clean title via Private Seller Exchange, 80K miles, 14.81 mi away. The N52 3.0L naturally-aspirated inline-6 is among BMW's most reliable modern engines.
picks.whatToVerify: Soft top operation, all four windows, heated glass rear window; headliner condition; oil leaks at valley pan and valve cover; rust at trunk lid and rear quarter seam; convertible top hydraulic system; DISA valve operation.
picks.bottomLine: The practical 4-seat drop-top choice — clean, reliable, close, and well-priced.

mustangs.introHtml: Ford Mustang convertibles within 35 miles, $7K–$15K. Three found across Cars.com and AutoTrader: a 1994 (44K mi, private seller), a 1995 (55K mi, dealer), and a 1987 Fox body (99K mi, Great Deal). All No Accidents + Clean Title.

picks.whyGood: NEW from Cars.com — 44,000 miles from a private seller in Eddington PA. An SN-95 GT with 44K miles is a rare survivor. The 5.0 HO V8 (215hp) sounds and feels like a real Mustang. Private seller often means no dealer markup and more honest condition disclosure. No accidents, clean title.
picks.whatToVerify: SN-95 convertible-specific: check frame rails at convertible top mount-to-rocker junction underneath for rust (very common); cowl rust; T-5 gearbox third-gear synchro; 8.8 rear axle clunk on takeoff; soft top fabric and rear plastic window. Ask seller for maintenance records.
picks.bottomLine: Low-mile private-seller SN-95 GT — frame rail inspection is the make-or-break check.

picks.whyGood: 55K verified miles on a 1995 GT with No Accidents — a genuine low-mileage SN-95 survivor. The 5.0 HO V8 is classic Mustang. At $10,495 it's priced as a usable driver, not an overpriced collector piece.
picks.whatToVerify: Same SN-95 checklist: frame rails at convertible top mount, cowl rust, T-5 third-gear synchro, 8.8 rear clunk, soft top fabric and rear window cracking.
picks.bottomLine: Rare low-mileage SN-95 GT at a dealer — compare frame rail condition carefully to the private-seller 1994 above.

picks.whyGood: Fox body GT at $9,500 with a Great Deal badge, No Accidents, clean title. The 1987 GT had the 5.0 HO making 225hp in classic Fox-body form. More raw and mechanical than SN-95. Great Deal badge from Cars.com suggests it's below market.
picks.whatToVerify: Fox body convertible rust at A-pillars, windshield frame, and lower rocker panels is pervasive — inspect carefully. Check soft top alignment, T-5 or AOD transmission condition, 8.8 rear axle, and all rubber seals.
picks.bottomLine: Raw Fox-body alternative at a fair price — rust inspection is the top priority on any 1987 convertible.
